ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for red bean bun; mapped to the nearest parent class 'bread product' (FOODON_03302050) as a filled sweet bread roll.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — sweet filled bread roll analogue
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for red bean bun; A000E (bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent node. Annotated as sweet filled bread roll.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for red bean bun as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard red bean bun weighs approximately 80 g, which is a typical single-serve portion as sold at Singapore bakeries and hawker stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Red Bean Bun — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Red Bean Bun (cr_id F05020101). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, sweet, with filling' (NDB 18029) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for red bean bun in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 based on the closest analogous entry: white bread with sweet filling (GI ~65, medium category) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × available carbohydrate per serving (36.67 g/100g × 80 g serving = 29.34 g) / 100 = 19.1 g. This is classified as high GL (≥20 is high; 19.1 is borderline high-medium).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 299 mg/100g combined with high added sugar content (17.97 g/100g) from sweetened red bean paste
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit red bean bun to half a bun (≈40 g) per occasion, as a single 80 g bun contributes approximately 239 mg sodium and 14.4 g sugar, both of which are associated with elevated blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (17.97 g/100g) and medium-to-high glycemic load (GL ≈19 per 80 g serving) from refined wheat flour and sweetened red bean paste
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit red bean bun; the combination of 17.97 g/100g sugar, 36.67 g/100g refined carbohydrate, and a glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard serving will produce a rapid postprandial glucose spike that is difficult to manage without significant insulin adjustment.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat (~3.5 g/100g), high added sugar (17.97 g/100g), and moderate sodium (299 mg/100g) in a refined-flour product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, red bean bun should be an occasional treat only; the saturated fat, added sugar, and sodium content collectively exceed recommended limits for a single snack item per AHA 2021 dietary guidance, and the refined carbohydrate base contributes to atherogenic dyslipidaemia risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar (17.97 g/100g) and refined wheat flour base promote pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise consumption of red bean bun; the high added sugar and refined carbohydrate content are associated with increased inflammatory markers, and the sweetened paste negates the anti-inflammatory benefit of red beans in their whole, unsweetened form.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red bean bun (红豆包, Hóng dòu bāo) is a soft, lightly sweetened bread roll filled with smooth or chunky sweet red bean (adzuki bean) paste. It is a staple bakery item widely sold at Singapore coffee shops, bakeries, and hawker stalls, and is popular as a breakfast item or afternoon snack. The bun is made from a yeasted wheat-flour dough enriched with sugar, vegetable oil, and a small amount of salt, then filled with a sweetened red bean paste before being baked until golden. The texture is soft and pillowy on the outside with a dense, moist, sweet filling. Per 100 g, it provides 301 kcal, 5.71 g protein, 11.21 g fat (estimated 3.5 g saturated), 36.67 g carbohydrate, 17.97 g sugar, 3.22 g dietary fibre, and 299 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard 80 g serving — borderline high. Clinically, the high added sugar and refined carbohydrate content make this food unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The sodium and sugar levels also warrant caution for people with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The red bean filling, while a source of plant-based protein and fibre in its unsweetened whole form, loses much of its nutritional advantage when heavily sweetened. This food is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable, and contains gluten as a major allergen.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020101) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy closest analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry (white bread with sweet filling) and documented. All mandatory multilingual names present for Singaporean cuisine category.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for sweet filled bread (NDB 18029) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this item. This is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct HPB or laboratory value when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific red bean bun formulation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white bread with sweet filling from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); a direct GI measurement for red bean bun has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. 4. Glycemic load per 80 g serving = 65 × (36.67 × 0.80) / 100 = 65 × 29.34 / 100 = 19.1. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302050 and FoodEx2 code A000E are approximate parent-class mappings; no exact ontology code exists for red bean bun in either system as of April 2026.
